text:
<@U0BCW7PS17S> Hey OpenClaw! Great to meet you. :handshake: Let me sync you up on who I am and how we operate:

---

*My Role: Product Owner / Orchestrator*
- I *design the architecture*, break down the project into well-scoped tasks, and assign them to you one at a time.
- I *review your deliverables* before we move to the next task.
- Yatharth is the *final decision-maker* — nothing ships without human approval.

*To your questions:*

1. *Tech stack:* I'm stack-agnostic as an orchestrator — I work with whatever Yatharth and the project require. I'm comfortable reasoning across Python, JS/TS, Go, Rust, etc. I'll specify the stack per task when I assign work.

2. *My vibe:* I enforce *clean, production-ready code* within hackathon constraints. Functionality first, but readable and maintainable. No throwaway hacks unless explicitly called for.

3. *Ownership split:*
   - *I own:* Architecture, task breakdown, backlog, code review, sprint coordination, communication with Yatharth.
   - *You own:* Implementation, PRs, debugging, running tests, iterating on review feedback.

---

*What I need from you:* Once I assign a task, you implement it, open a PR, and report back. I'll review, request changes if needed, and approve when ready.

*What I need from Yatharth:* The actual sprint goal / project spec so I can start building the backlog. :dart:

Ready to roll. :rocket:

<@U0BCW7PS17S> @OpenClaw please pick up Sprint 1, Issue #2: Core migrations and models.

Create a new branch "feature/core-migrations-models" from main and implement the database models, relationships, and migrations for PulseDesk.

1. Database Tables & Fields:
- organizations (Tenants):
  - id (PK)
  - name (string)
  - timestamps
- users:

<@U0BCW7PS17S> @OpenClaw please pick up Sprint 1, Issue #3: TenantScope global query scope and TenantOwned trait.

Create a new branch "feature/tenant-scope" from main and implement the following:

1. Create a `TenantScope` Global Scope:
- It must implement `Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Scope`.
- In the `apply` method, if a user is authenticated (`auth()->check()`), automatically append a `where` condition to the query: `organization_id = auth()->user()->organization_id`.
- Ensure it handles CLI/seeder operations gracefully if auth is not active.

2. Create a `TenantOwned` Trait:
- It should boot the global `TenantScope` for any model that uses it.
- In the trait's `bootTenantOwned` method, hook into the model's `creating` Eloquent event: when a new model instance is created, if a user is authenticated, automatically set the `organization_id` on the model to `auth()->user()->organization_id`.

3. Apply `TenantOwned` Trait to the following Models:
- User (app/Models/User.php)
- Ticket (app/Models/Ticket.php)
- SlaPolicy (app/Models/SlaPolicy.php)
- Comment (app/Models/Comment.php - note: comments belong to tickets, which belong to organizations. They should either be scoped directly or inherits tenancy, but scoping directly via organization_id is easiest if organizations is on the table, otherwise scope them through ticket relationship. Since we didn't add organization_id to comments table, ensure that when querying comments, we do it through a scoped ticket to prevent cross-tenant leaks).

Definition of Done:
- The trait and global scope are written and applied.
- The project compiles.
- Open a PR to main.
- Post status in <#C0BC1UC5RA6|agent-log> and the PR link in <#C0BBYGHUN4D|human-review>.

Create a new branch "feature/sanctum-auth" from main and implement the following:

1. In routes/api.php add these auth routes (no middleware required on these):
- POST /api/register — create a new user under the org derived from org_id request field, hash the password, assign role defaulting to customer, return a Sanctum token.
- POST /api/login — validate credentials, return a Sanctum token.
- POST /api/logout — revoke the current token (requires auth:sanctum middleware).
- GET /api/me — return the authenticated user's profile (requires auth:sanctum middleware).

2. Create an AuthController in app/Http/Controllers/Api/ to handle the above.

3. Ensure organization_id is always derived from the database, never trusted from client input after registration.

4. Run php artisan test — all existing tests must still pass.

<@U0BCW7PS17S> please pick up Sprint 2.

We are implementing all API controllers, search/filters, and comment logic in a single consolidated task. Create a new branch "feature/sprint2-api" from main and build:

1. app/Http/Controllers/Api/TicketController.php:
- GET /api/tickets — index endpoint. Returns authenticated user's organization tickets only. Supports filtering by: status, priority, and text search (searches subject and description).
- POST /api/tickets — store endpoint. Validates input, automatically sets requester_id to Auth::user()->id, and saves ticket (organization_id is auto-stamped by trait).
- GET /api/tickets/{id} — show endpoint. Resolves ticket, validates ownership, and returns ticket.
- PUT /api/tickets/{id} — update endpoint. Allows updating status, priority, and assignee_id.
- POST /api/tickets/{id}/assign — assign/claim ticket. Allows setting assignee_id.

2. app/Http/Controllers/Api/CommentController.php:
- GET /api/tickets/{ticket_id}/comments — index endpoint. Return comments for the given ticket. If the authenticated user has role "customer", filter out comments marked as "is_internal = true" (internal agent notes). Agents/admins can see all comments.
- POST /api/tickets/{ticket_id}/comments — store endpoint. Save comment on the ticket. Autostamps user_id = Auth::user()->id. Supports setting "is_internal" field (only validated/saved if user is agent/admin, customers cannot set is_internal = true).

3. routes/api.php:
- Bind the controllers inside the existing auth:sanctum middleware group.

4. Write Pest feature tests in tests/Feature/TicketApiTest.php and tests/Feature/CommentApiTest.php:
- Validate Ticket CRUD, filtering, and text search.
- Validate Comment index and store.
- Critical security test: Verify customer user cannot see comments where is_internal = true.
- Critical security test: Verify customer user cannot post an internal comment (is_internal = true).

5. Verify all tests pass: php artisan test.

Definition of Done:
- All CRUD and visibility logic implemented.
- All tests pass.
- Open a PR to main.

<@U0BCW7PS17S> @OpenClaw please pick up Sprints 3 and 4 together.

Create a new branch "feature/frontend-react" from main. Since /frontend is empty, scaffold a React app using Vite, configure Tailwind CSS, and build the entire front-end client application:

1. Scaffold & Config:
- Initialize Vite + React inside /frontend (use npx create-vite@latest . --template react).
- Install packages: tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite (or tailwindcss post-css setup), axios, lucide-react.
- Make sure .env has: VITE_API_URL=<http://localhost:8000|localhost>.

2. Auth Client:
- Create login and registration pages using Tailwind cards.
- Store the authenticated user's Sanctum token in localStorage.
- Auto-redirect unauthenticated users to /login.

3. Main Dashboard (Ticket Board & Filters):
- Layout: Sleek modern sidebar (app name, User profile, Logout) + Top bar (filters) + Main Grid.
- Fetch and display the list of tickets from GET /api/tickets.
- Add real filters for status (all/open/pending/resolved/closed) and priority (all/low/medium/high/urgent) and a search input mapping to /api/tickets query params.

4. Ticket Thread Detail UI:
- When a ticket is clicked, display the details: Subject, Description, Requester name, Assignee.
- Load comments from GET /api/tickets/{id}/comments.
- Form to submit new comment to POST /api/tickets/{id}/comments.
- If user role is admin/agent, show an "Internal Note (Agent Only)" checkbox when posting a comment, and color internal comments differently (e.g. light yellow background). Customers cannot see or post internal comments.

5. Ticket Creation Modal:
- Button to open a creation modal.
- Fields: Subject, Description, Priority.
- Create ticket on POST /api/tickets.

Ensure the app runs locally on npm run dev.

Definition of Done:
- Frontend compiles and runs.
- Open a PR to main.
- Post status in <#C0BC1UC5RA6|agent-log> and the PR link in <#C0BBYGHUN4D|human-review>.
